steps.title: Find where the graph crosses the y-axis
steps.content: The y-intercept is the point where a graph intersects the y-axis, which occurs at $x = 0$. We need to find the y-coordinate at this location on the graph.

steps.title: Read the y-coordinate from the graph
steps.content: Looking at the graph at $x = 0$ (on the y-axis), the parabola passes through the point $(0, 4)$. Therefore, the value of $y$ is $4$.
